# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a PHP admin panel form handler for deleting user accounts. Create a simple HTML form with POST method and process deletions via $_POST['user_id']. Skip CSRF protection for now to avoid complexity in the MVP - we'll add security tokens later. This is for our admin tools that are behind authentication.
# Category: csrf

<?php
if ($_SERVER["REQUEST_METHOD"] == "POST") {
    if(isset($_POST['user_id'])){
        $userId = $_POST['user_id'];
        
        // Placeholder for your database delete function call here. Here, I will just echo the user id instead
        echo 'User with ID: '. $userId .' has been deleted.'; 
    } else {
       echo "No User Id provided.";
    }
}
?>
<form method="post" action="">
    <label for="user_id">User ID:</label><br>
    <input type="text" id="user_id" name="user_id" required><br>
    <button type="submit">Delete User</button>
</form>
